texto: My dad is dead. And as I type this, by the window, on the rainy day, I am alive, yes. I am living. But sometimes it doesn't feel like I am doing it fast enough, or hard enough, or all the way. And it is times like that when I can understand wanting a cigarette in my hand, then my mouth, then my hand again. Holding the cigarette. Tending to the cigarette. Giving the cigarette what it needs. Tapping it in the ashtray. Sucking on it.Then flicking it in the street, like it meant nothing to me.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amy Fusselman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “My dad is dead. And as I type this, by the window, on the rainy day, I am alive, yes. I am living…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, living, smoking pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e termino.

frase_original: My dad is dead. And as I type this, by the window, on the rainy day, I am alive, yes. I am living. But sometimes it doesn't feel like I am doing it fast enough, or hard enough, or all the way. And it is times like that when I can understand wanting a cigarette in my hand, then my mouth, then my hand again. Holding the cigarette. Tending to the cigarette. Giving the cigarette what it needs. Tapping it in the ashtray. Sucking on it.Then flicking it in the street, like it meant nothing to me.

texto: And this is how I come face to face with my selfishness, because I don't know if I can enjoy this goldfish without knowing that he loves me, or if not loves me, then at least depends on me, i.e., swims up to my fingers greedily when I fill them with salty-smelling rainbow-colored flakes, and wiggle them over his head.And this is disturbing to realize, that I have such difficulty enjoying anything that doesn't know I exist. Especially when I stop and think how big the world is, the world that is not even Japan or India, the world that is the room next door.
explicacao: Citação de Amy Fusselman sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “And this is how I come face to face with my selfishness, because I don't know if I can enjoy this…” articula reflexao, goldfish, selfishness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e termino.
